findings.evidence: `restrict` is defined at lines 164-168 and `holderSize` at lines 190-191; `rg "restrict_.*(supNorm|holderSeminorm|holderSize)|(supNorm|holderSeminorm|holderSize)_.*restrict" TauCeti/Analysis/Holder/Basic.lean` returns no API.
findings.fix: Add restriction estimates such as `supNorm_restrict_le`, `holderSeminorm_restrict_le`, and `holderSize_restrict_le` so users can use restrictions in estimates without unfolding the carrier or Mathlib seminorm definitions.
findings.issue: `restrict` is exposed as part of the domain API, but only `restrict_apply` is provided; there is no public lemma controlling `supNorm`, `holderSeminorm`, or `holderSize` after restriction.

summary: The algebraic operations now have size estimates, but the advertised restriction-to-domain API is still missing the characteristic size control needed by downstream estimates.
